title: poa jansenii
intro: Poa jansenii, also known as Jansen's fescue, is a type of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. It is native to the mountainous regions of New Zealand and has been used for centuries in traditional medicine. The plant has been found to have medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been used to treat various health conditions such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Poa jansenii include the ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and improve immune function, and it can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and topical creams for its medicinal uses.
